channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Justices to hear arguments on gender-affirming care for minors
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A list of mispronounced words provides a retrospective of 2024, from Kamala to Chappell
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and breakout pop star Chappell Roan were among the year's most talked-about people. Their names were also among the most mispronounced.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Memphis may sue Kia, Hyundai over car thefts and rent
item.description: Memphis is considering filing lawsuits against Hyundai and Kia over the costs of an "epidemic of vehicle thefts.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Transgender rights case lands at Supreme Court amid debate over ban on medical treatments for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is hearing arguments Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, which is a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in NYC: Sources
item.description: The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a Midtown shooting Wednesday morning, sources have confirmed to Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KREX 5

item.title: Rescuers reassess the safety of their search for a woman they believe fell into a sinkhole
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — Rescuers contemplated the safest way Wednesday to search for a woman who apparently fell into a Pennsylvania sinkhole while looking for her lost cat, saying a crumbling old coal mine was complicating efforts and endangering workers.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and GENE PUSKAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Trump may replace Hegseth with DeSantis: WSJ
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is rumored to be considering replacing his Defense Secretary pick, former Fox News personality Pete Hegseth, with Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Missouri executes a man for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl in 2007
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — Rowan Ford knew Christopher Collings as “Uncle Chris” after he spent several months living with her family. On Tuesday, Collings was executed for sexually assaulting and killing the child, then dumping her body in a sinkhole outside a small Missouri town.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chinese man living illegally in California tried to send weapons, ammo to North Korea: DOJ
item.description: Shenghua Wen, 41, was charged with conspiracy to violate the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the United States Department of Justice said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trial begins in the killing of an Ole Miss student who was known in the local LGBTQ+ community
item.description: A University of Mississippi student who disappeared in 2022 was a happy person who spoke with his mother daily and called her every year to sing happy birthday, his mom testified Tuesday in the trial of the man charged with killing her son.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors began deliberating and soon revisited some of their legal instructions Tuesday in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a New York subway rider whose behavior was alarming other passengers.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chad Chronister, Donald Trump’s pick to run the DEA, withdraws name from consideration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, said Tuesday he was withdrawing his name from consideration, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being nominated for a position requiring Senate confirmati
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Duty to intervene charges dismissed for 2 officers in Christian Glass' death
item.description: Body-worn camera footage of the encounter shows that deputies broke a window of his vehicle, shot him with bean bags and used a Taser against him.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel luring chemistry students to make fentanyl: Report
item.description: Recruitment of chemistry students at Mexican universities is part of the Sinaloa cartel's strategy to expand its fentanyl empire.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Former Abercrombie CEO's partner pleads not guilty to sex trafficking charges, is freed on bond
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — Matthew Smith, the partner of former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, was released on $10 million bond Tuesday in their sex trafficking case with conditions including home detention and GPS monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California cuts back on water deliveries, anticipating dry year
item.description: The State Water Project is planning for a dry 2025 punctuated by extreme storms like we’ve seen in late November,” Karla Nemeth said,
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Infant thrown from car during speedy Ohio pursuit; driver arrested
item.description: It took an hour for the infant to be found on the highway in below-freezing weather, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Lydia Reuille, NewsNation

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: TikTok influencer Marlena Velez arrested, accused of theft for 2nd time in weeks: police
item.description: Marlena Velez was previously accused of stealing $500 worth of items from Target and posting incriminating video to social media.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Babies found safe after car stolen and infants dumped in cold, IMPD says
item.description: Shortly before noon, officers located the stolen vehicle. The babies were not inside and the suspect was gone.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Company provides insurance against stolen delivery packages
item.description: PorchPals costs $120 per year, covering up to $2,000 in stolen packages or three claims annually. Monthly billing has a rate of $15.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, WGN 9

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Estranged husband accused of murder after missing woman's body found
item.description: A man is facing a second-degree murder charge a week after his wife, who filed for divorce in Clackamas County recently, was reported missing.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Salk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, ABC 4

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
